items.title: SpaceX files FCC request for 100,000 Starlink satellites
items.summary: A Wall Street analyst says a constellation this size would give Starlink “massive headroom" to compete, creating significant disruption for wireline broadband operators.

items.title: Is SpaceX developing a mobile phone now too?
items.summary: Between a new report about a possible Starlink AI device and ongoing speculation about Elon Musk’s terrestrial network aspirations, SpaceX is shaking up the wireless industry like nobody’s business.

items.title: Open RAN deployments hinge on multi-vendor interoperability
items.summary: Interoperability across multi-vendor systems has emerged as the central challenge shaping open RAN deployments.

items.title: Dish’s bankruptcy exposes the messy aftermath of its 5G gamble
items.summary: Big public tower companies and smaller individual landlords are left holding the bag after Dish Wireless filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy proceedings.

items.title: Inside AT&T's industrial IoT logistics play
items.summary: AT&T is leveraging its Wiliot partnership to build its logistics business. And connectivity is just part of it.

items.title: T-Mobile reshuffles executive ranks as Mike Katz exits
items.summary: T-Mobile’s Mike Katz is leaving. Chris Sambar is joining as chief enterprise officer and André Almeida is getting an expanded position as chief marketing, brand and broadband officer.

items.title: Korean operators ramp up AI investments to make country an AI superpower
items.summary: Korean telcos are racing to turn AI ambition into infrastructure reality, with SK Telecom, KT and LG Uplus pouring billions into data centers, compute and connectivity as South Korea pushes to become a top-three global AI power.

items.title: Opinion: Despite obstacles, mobile and cloud are destined for a meet-cute
items.summary: Mobile network software is quickly becoming a stand-alone business that is not tied to hardware.

items.title: Verizon EVP Joe Russo says if the network is ‘invisible,’ it’s working
items.summary: As Russo, who began his career at Bell Atlantic 30 years ago, prepares to retire from Verizon, he reflects on his career, technology changes and talks about the challenges ahead.

items.title: SoftBank creates neocloud to serve US enterprises
items.summary: The new SB Neo business will leverage the 10-gigawatt-scale energy and AI infrastructure SoftBank currently has under development.
